# Hitbox

> Highly customizable async caching framework for Rust designed for high-performance applications.

Hitbox is an open-source cache orchestrator for Rust. Protocol-agnostic async core with first-class HTTP support. Pluggable backends from in-memory to distributed. Built on Tower, works with any tokio-based service.

Hitbox supports two caching approaches: middleware (a Tower layer wraps handlers transparently) and function memoization (`#[cached]` macro on async functions). Both keep caching logic out of business code.

## Key features

- Tower middleware for Axum and any tower-based framework
- Client-side caching for Hyper and Reqwest
- Function memoization with `#[cached]` macro and derive-based key extraction
- Pluggable backends — Moka (in-memory), Redis (distributed), FeOxDB (embedded persistent)
- Composable backends — L1/L2/L3 tiers with configurable read, write, and refill policies
- Dogpile prevention — configurable concurrency limit per key with broadcast
- Stale-while-revalidate — serve stale data while refreshing in background (OffloadRevalidate)
- Predicates — control what gets cached by method, path, headers, query, body, status code, JQ expressions
- Extractors — build cache keys from method, path segments, query params, headers, body (regex, JQ, hash)
- Serialization — Bincode, Rkyv (zero-copy), RON, JSON
- Compression — zstd, gzip
- Observability — cache hit/miss/stale counters, latency histograms, backend I/O, offload task metrics
- YAML configuration — define caching setup in config files, change rules at runtime

## Performance

- 172x throughput improvement with 50ms simulated backend (970 → 167,270 req/s)
- ~20µs overhead on cache miss, ~10-12µs on cache hit
- Moka read: ~2µs (5KB), Redis read: ~160µs (5KB)

## Project structure

| Crate | Role |
|-------|------|
| `hitbox` | Main crate: policy configuration, stale cache, feature flags |
| `hitbox-core` | Core traits (Predicate, Extractor) for protocol-agnostic caching |
| `hitbox-backend` | Backend trait and utilities |
| `hitbox-fn` | Function memoization with `#[cached]` macro |
| `hitbox-derive` | Derive macros (KeyExtract, CacheableResponse, CacheableRequest) |
| `hitbox-http` | HTTP predicates and extractors |
| `hitbox-tower` | Tower middleware (Cache layer) |
| `hitbox-configuration` | YAML/file-based configuration |
| `hitbox-moka` | Moka in-memory backend |
| `hitbox-redis` | Redis distributed backend |
| `hitbox-feoxdb` | FeOxDB embedded persistent backend |
| `hitbox-reqwest` | Reqwest client-side caching via reqwest-middleware |

## Links

- Blog: https://blog.hitbox.rs
- GitHub: https://github.com/hit-box/hitbox
- Docs: https://docs.rs/hitbox
- Crates.io: https://crates.io/crates/hitbox
- Examples: https://github.com/hit-box/examples

## Blog articles

- [Add Response Caching to Axum in 10 Minutes](https://blog.hitbox.rs/blog/axum-caching-in-10-minutes/): Step-by-step guide to adding transparent HTTP response caching to Axum using Hitbox.
- [Should I Use Moka or Hitbox?](https://blog.hitbox.rs/blog/moka-or-hitbox/): Overview of the Rust caching ecosystem — what stores data, what coordinates it, and where Hitbox fits in.
